claim: JASON-OS session-begin skill is identical to SoNash v2.0 but cannot execute its core context-loading phase because SESSION_CONTEXT.md is absent from JASON-OS
evidence: Both repos have session-begin/SKILL.md v2.0 identical line-for-line; filesystem confirms SESSION_CONTEXT.md missing from JASON-OS root

claim: session-end skill is entirely absent from JASON-OS; BOOTSTRAP_DEFERRED.md records the reason as Phase 3 dependency on npm scripts that do not exist in JASON-OS
evidence: JASON-OS .claude/skills/ directory listing confirms absence; BOOTSTRAP_DEFERRED.md lines 14-29 state reason

claim: SoNash has 28 production hooks wired across 7 event types; JASON-OS has 4 active hooks across 2 event types (SessionStart and PreToolUse)
verificationNote: Core gap confirmed (4 vs 28 hooks, 2 vs 7 event types). Original parenthetical 'PreToolUse only' was wrong — SessionStart is also wired. Claim text corrected.
evidence: SoNash settings.json 317-line full hook table; JASON-OS settings.json 81-line minimal wiring; V1a filesystem verification

claim: SoNash statusline (Go binary, 22 widgets) writes a bridge file to /tmp/claude-ctx-{session_id}.json that gsd-context-monitor.js reads to inject context pressure warnings into Claude's context — a two-hook coordination pattern absent from JASON-OS
evidence: gsd-statusline.js source; gsd-context-monitor.js source; JASON-OS statusline-command.sh has no bridge file write

claim: SoNash has a 3-layer compaction defense (commit-tracker, pre-compaction-save, compact-restore); JASON-OS has none of the three layers — only manual checkpoint
evidence: SoNash settings.json PreCompact and SessionStart hooks; pre-compaction-save.js and compact-restore.js source files; JASON-OS settings.json confirms none present

claim: JASON-OS MEMORY.md has 25 feedback entries (vs SoNash 37, not 11 as originally stated); 1 project file vs 27 in SoNash. Every session knows HOW to work with Jason but not WHAT Jason is working on
verificationNote: JASON-OS counts correct (25 feedback, 1 project). SoNash feedback count was wrong: 37 actual (not 11). SoNash project count ~27 confirmed. Behavioral insight holds.
evidence: Filesystem inspection of both memory directories; type distribution counts verified by V1a

claim: The /todo and /checkpoint skills are identical between SoNash and JASON-OS in skill definitions, but JASON-OS lacks the session-start hook that surfaces todo count on startup
evidence: Both todo/SKILL.md and checkpoint/SKILL.md files confirmed identical; session-start hook absent from JASON-OS

claim: SoNash's trigger architecture has 6 strata (CLAUDE.md table, UserPromptSubmit NLP, PreToolUse conditionals, PostToolUse cascades, PostToolUseFailure loop detector, agent PROACTIVELY clauses); JASON-OS has only 2 (CLAUDE.md table stubs, PreToolUse guards)
evidence: SoNash settings.json lines 71-306; JASON-OS settings.json lines 48-79; CLAUDE.md comparison; agent directory inspection

claim: user-prompt-handler.js (~720 LOC) fires on every user message and dispatches 6 sub-functions: guardrails injection, frustration detection, alerts reminder, skill-directive emission (turning PRE-TASK table from advisory to enforced), session-end nudge, and plan complexity detection
verificationNote: Verified as present in SoNash. Portability confidence downgraded HIGH→MEDIUM per CH-C-004: runAnalyze() emits directives for agents that don't exist in JASON-OS — requires line-level extraction before wiring.
evidence: SoNash user-prompt-handler.js full read lines 44-718; settings.json registration lines 271-281

claim: SoNash PostToolUse has 9-10 matchers creating cascading tripwires including a cross-hook state loop: track-agent-invocation writes .session-agents.json which pre-commit-agent-compliance reads to block commits if required agents were not invoked
evidence: SoNash settings.json lines 161-270; pre-commit-agent-compliance.js lines 64-91; track-agent-invocation.js lines 221-231

claim: loop-detector.js (PostToolUseFailure) hashes normalized build/test failure output and warns Claude to change approach after 3 identical failures within a 20-minute window; JASON-OS has no equivalent
evidence: SoNash settings.json lines 292-307; loop-detector.js lines 1-24, 280-291; JASON-OS settings.json confirms absence

claim: 17 SoNash agents carry PROACTIVELY clauses in their frontmatter that cause Claude to self-dispatch; 0 of JASON-OS's 8 agents have PROACTIVELY clauses
evidence: Agent frontmatter reads for code-reviewer, security-auditor, explore; JASON-OS agents directory listing

claim: The brainstorm -> deep-research -> deep-plan -> execute workflow chain is advisory in JASON-OS (stated in CLAUDE.md and memory) but has no hook enforcement; in SoNash it is enforced by user-prompt-handler.js runAnalyze() which emits mandatory PRE-TASK directives
verificationNote: Verified. Portability of runAnalyze() enforcement downgraded MEDIUM per CH-C-004 and CH-C-008. During exploratory JASON-OS phase, advisory rules may be more appropriate than mandatory enforcement.
evidence: SoNash CLAUDE.md lines 127-170; JASON-OS CLAUDE.md lines 86-98; user-prompt-handler.js lines 280-430

claim: Both SoNash and JASON-OS settings.json carry identical permission deny rules (4 rules: no force push, no push to main, no hard reset, no rm -rf) and identical env vars (AGENT_TEAMS=1, GLOB_TIMEOUT=60)
evidence: settings.json lines 17-21 both repos; env block confirmed identical

claim: JASON-OS settings-guardian.js and large-file-gate.js both reference scripts/lib/sanitize-error.cjs at a path that requires verification — if the resolved path does not exist, both hooks crash on load affecting every Write, Edit, and Read operation
refutedReason: scripts/lib/sanitize-error.cjs EXISTS at JASON-OS/scripts/lib/sanitize-error.cjs. Both hooks are functioning. The stated precondition (path does not exist) is false.
evidence: JASON-OS settings-guardian.js line 21; large-file-gate.js line 23; path analysis from .claude/hooks/ to scripts/lib/

claim: SoNash has 29 hook scripts (plus lib/ and global/ subdirs); JASON-OS has 4 hooks wired. The 5 highest-value portable hooks missing are: pre-compaction-save, compact-restore, commit-tracker, post-read-handler, loop-detector
evidence: SoNash settings.json full hook table; D2a portability classification table; JASON-OS settings.json

claim: SoNash uses bash .claude/hooks/ensure-fnm.sh node <hook>.js wrapper for every hook invocation to guarantee correct Node version; JASON-OS calls node directly with no version guarantee
evidence: SoNash settings.json hook command strings; ensure-fnm.sh source; JASON-OS settings.json hook commands

claim: SoNash statusline Go binary has 22 widgets including active task, context gauge, rate limits, weather, GitHub PR status, CI/CD pipeline, and session counter. JASON-OS bash statusline shows path, git branch, node version, model, and context percentage only
evidence: tools/statusline/widgets.go lines 26-48; config.toml; JASON-OS statusline-command.sh

claim: JASON-OS settings.json includes MCP allow rules for servers that don't exist (no .mcp.json, no installed plugins). The allow list references sonarcloud, memory, sequential-thinking, context7, episodic-memory — all dead weight until servers are registered
evidence: JASON-OS settings.json allow block; .mcp.json absence confirmed; installed_plugins.json shows empty plugins

claim: No keybindings.json exists in either SoNash or JASON-OS. Operator interaction is entirely through slash commands and built-in terminal UI shortcuts — keybindings are not a configured organ
evidence: Exhaustive search of both repos and user-global settings confirmed no keybindings.json

claim: SoNash CLAUDE.md v6.0 uses [GATE: mechanism] and [BEHAVIORAL: method] enforcement annotations on every rule. JASON-OS CLAUDE.md v0.1 has zero enforcement annotations — every rule implicitly reads as behavioral-only even when hooks exist to enforce it
evidence: SoNash CLAUDE.md lines throughout; JASON-OS CLAUDE.md full read; D2c Finding 5

claim: SoNash CLAUDE.md Section 8 has 11 declared docs (not 5 as originally stated); one-hop traversal reaches ~25 documents across 4 tiers. JASON-OS CLAUDE.md Section 8 is TBD with no reference doc ecosystem
verificationNote: SoNash Section 8 table has 11 data rows confirmed by V1b grep count (not 5 as originally claimed). One-hop traversal ~25 total is plausible. JASON-OS TBD confirmed. Claim text corrected.
evidence: SoNash CLAUDE.md Section 8; AI_WORKFLOW.md link traversal; JASON-OS CLAUDE.md section 8

claim: SoNash has 80 skills; JASON-OS has 9. 9 SoNash skills are already ported. Of the remaining 71, approximately 32 are fully portable (no sanitization), ~28 need sanitization, and ~11 are non-portable (SoNash-specific)
evidence: skills/ directory listings both repos; grep-based reference counting across all SKILL.md files

claim: session-end v2.2 has 4 phases, 10+ steps. Phase 3 (Steps 7a-7d) contains ALL SoNash-specific blockers. Phases 1, 2, and 4 (plus Step 10 inlined) are fully portable. A v0 session-end stripping Phase 3 and inlining commits preserves the essential home-feel ritual
evidence: session-end SKILL.md v2.2 full read; session-end-commit.js inspection (confirmed no SoNash deps)

claim: user-prompt-handler.js contains a farewell detector (runSessionEnd function) that injects a session-end reminder when it detects goodbye phrases. This is 100% portable pure string regex matching and gives the session-end ritual its ambient nudge
verificationNote: farewell detector confirmed portable. Overall user-prompt-handler portability downgraded to MEDIUM due to runAnalyze() GSD-specific coupling (CH-C-004).
evidence: user-prompt-handler.js source analysis; settings.json UserPromptSubmit registration

claim: The /todo skill in JASON-OS is completely non-functional. Its SKILL.md mandates scripts/planning/todos-cli.js for every mutation, but todos-cli.js, render-todos.js, todos-mutations.js, and .planning/todos.jsonl do not exist in JASON-OS
evidence: JASON-OS todo/SKILL.md line 27 mandate; filesystem confirms scripts/planning/ directory is absent from JASON-OS

claim: JASON-OS has no .claude/teams/ directory. SoNash has audit-review-team.md and research-plan-team.md. The research-plan-team is directly relevant to the brainstorm → deep-research → deep-plan pipeline
evidence: JASON-OS directory listing confirms absence; SoNash .claude/teams/ listing and content confirms both files

claim: deep-research Phase 5 (hasDebtCandidates routing) routes to /add-debt which does not exist in JASON-OS. This creates a dead-end in every research session. The dead-end is low-severity (last menu item, does not corrupt output) but surfaces on every session
evidence: JASON-OS deep-research/SKILL.md lines 332-333 (identical to SoNash); JASON-OS skills directory confirms no add-debt

claim: hasDebtCandidates appears in both top-level and consumerHints of actual metadata.json files, but REFERENCE.md template shows it only in consumerHints. Writing both locations is the safer choice for JASON-OS porting
evidence: t28 and multi-layer-memory metadata.json files; deep-research REFERENCE.md schema

claim: pr-review v4.6 is a feedback-processor (processes pasted review input), not a review generator. Its core 8-step protocol, DAS framework, parallel agent strategy, and state persistence are all fully portable — the SoNash coupling is in the tool-specific integrations (SonarCloud, TDMS, Gemini)
evidence: pr-review SKILL.md v4.6 full read; portability classification table

claim: pr-retro v4.8 has a hard gate (Critical Rule #10): retro is blocked until every action item is implemented or user explicitly says defer. retros.jsonl has 71 records showing this gate produces real code changes — action items with status complete dominate later entries
evidence: pr-retro SKILL.md v4.8; retros.jsonl 71 entries inspected

claim: pr-ecosystem-audit dependency in BOOTSTRAP_DEFERRED.md overstates the pr-retro blocker. Actual SKILL.md shows pr-ecosystem-audit appears only in routing header, not in any step. The 9-step pipeline is independently executable
evidence: pr-retro SKILL.md line-by-line inspection vs BOOTSTRAP_DEFERRED.md claim

claim: JASON-OS scripts/lib has 7 of ~21 SoNash lib entries. Key missing items with skill impact: todos-mutations.js (blocks /todo), validate-skip-reason.js (CLAUDE.md §4.14 enforcement), safe-cas-io.js, validate-paths.js
evidence: JASON-OS scripts/lib/ directory listing; SoNash scripts/lib/ directory listing

claim: AI_WORKFLOW.md (31KB, SoNash root) is the master session navigation document for AI assistants. JASON-OS has no equivalent. Without it, Claude navigates JASON-OS by memory or filesystem enumeration rather than consulting a structured guide
evidence: SoNash AI_WORKFLOW.md header and size; JASON-OS root directory listing confirms absence

claim: JASON-OS has no .nvmrc Node version pin. Since hooks call node directly without fnm wrapper, a new environment could silently use the wrong Node version. One-line fix with outsized portability benefit
evidence: JASON-OS root directory listing; SoNash .nvmrc confirmed; ensure-fnm.sh wrapper pattern in SoNash

claim: AutoDream (autoDreamEnabled: true in ~/.claude/settings.json) is active and operates in-session, not as a background daemon. It is the write path for auto-generated memory. JASON-OS inherits this setting from global config
evidence: Global settings.json autoDreamEnabled flag; multi-layer-memory research user confirmation; Claude Code version 2.1.109 confirmed

claim: SoNash settings-guardian.js in JASON-OS lists pre-commit-agent-compliance.js as a CRITICAL_HOOK but that hook is not wired in JASON-OS settings.json — creating a false-positive guard condition
refutedReason: JASON-OS settings-guardian.js CRITICAL_HOOKS array contains only block-push-to-main.js and settings-guardian.js. pre-commit-agent-compliance.js is NOT in the list. The ported version was already cleaned up.
evidence: JASON-OS settings-guardian.js CRITICAL_HOOKS list; JASON-OS settings.json PreToolUse section

claim: SoNash's session-begin SKILL.md references health scripts (npm run patterns:check, npm run session:gaps, npm run hooks:health) that do not exist in JASON-OS — the skill will fail or skip Phase 3 health checks on invocation
evidence: session-begin SKILL.md Phase 3 references; JASON-OS package.json / scripts directory absence

claim: The memory system auto-injects MEMORY.md (first 200 lines) and CLAUDE.md on every turn. Topic files are loaded on-demand. This always-on injection is the mechanism that creates the home-feel of behavioral consistency across sessions without any operator action
verificationNote: Filesystem confirms MEMORY.md and CLAUDE.md exist. The 200-line limit constant and always-on injection mechanism are internal Claude Code runtime behaviors not verifiable from filesystem. Downgraded HIGH→MEDIUM per V2b verdict.
evidence: Official Claude Code memory docs; Claude Code binary memory limit constant; filesystem inspection of both memory systems

claim: SoNash CLAUDE.md Section 7 has a prior art scan trigger: for any build task, scan .research/EXTRACTIONS.md for prior art. JASON-OS has no EXTRACTIONS.md or equivalent. Without it, every new thing gets built from scratch rather than reusing extracted patterns
evidence: SoNash CLAUDE.md line 131 (confirmed ~line 133); .research/EXTRACTIONS.md (624 lines); JASON-OS CLAUDE.md Section 7

claim: pre-commit-fixer skill (Value 9/10, fully portable) is referenced in CLAUDE.md guardrail #9 but does not exist in JASON-OS — creating a guardrail that points to a nonexistent skill
evidence: JASON-OS CLAUDE.md §4 guardrail #9 text; JASON-OS skills directory listing confirms absence; D2e portability rating

claim: The SoNash TDMS (Technical Debt Management System) consists of 28 scripts, MASTER_DEBT.jsonl with 8,505 items, a CI workflow, and 2 skills (add-debt, debt-runner). The portable core (excluding SonarCloud sync and roadmap scripts) is approximately 20 scripts. JASON-OS v0 minimum is 5 components to unblock deep-research routing
evidence: scripts/debt/ directory (28 scripts); MASTER_DEBT.jsonl head; add-debt SKILL.md; debt-runner SKILL.md

claim: The gsd-statusline.js active task display pulls from Claude's native todos dir (~/.claude/todos/{session_id}-agent-*.json), not from .planning/todos.jsonl. Statusline task display and /todo skill operate on different data stores
evidence: gsd-statusline.js source analysis noting two distinct todo data paths

claim: SoNash settings.local.json is a 40-entry accumulated policy log of every exceptional operation approved (including 4 SKIP_REASON push bypasses). JASON-OS settings.local.json has 1 entry (bootstrap cp command). This delta tells the maturity story: SoNash is session 281; JASON-OS is session 1
evidence: Both settings.local.json files inspected

claim: governance-logger.js (PostToolUse Write/Edit on CLAUDE.md or settings.json) creates an audit trail in governance-changes.jsonl and appends a hook warning. Fully portable, zero SoNash dependencies. Absent from JASON-OS
evidence: governance-logger.js source; settings.json wiring; JASON-OS settings.json confirms absence

claim: AgentSkills open standard (published 2025-12-18, 16-tool coverage as of 2026-03) was not evaluated during research. If compatible with SKILL.md, JASON-OS skills could become natively cross-tool at zero additional authoring cost.
verificationNote: New claim from OTB gap pursuit (CH-O-001). Sources are external URLs not filesystem-verified. Feasibility spike required before deep-plan finalizes skill authoring conventions.
evidence: CH-O-001 challenge; agentskills.io/home; anthropic.com/engineering/equipping-agents-for-the-real-world-with-agent-skills

claim: Adding PROACTIVELY clauses to JASON-OS agent frontmatter is the highest-leverage single porting action: pure text edits to 8 existing files, zero infra, 30-minute cost, converts agents from tools-to-invoke to colleagues-who-self-dispatch
verificationNote: Derived from CH-O-006 OTB challenge. Grounded in V1a-verified C-012 (0 PROACTIVELY clauses in JASON-OS vs 17 in SoNash). New claim adds the leverage characterization.
evidence: CH-O-006; C-012 verified absence; SoNash agent frontmatter pattern confirmed
